dbEnc.description: Configuration for application side database encryption. Use recommended only if you have to run in a trusted execution environment (German VAU).

enabled.description: Enables/disables application side database encryption. Use recommended only if you have to run in a trusted execution environment (German VAU).

bootstrapInterval.description: Interval in which to check for encrypted realm. Mainly interesting for the first boot during installation. (This value is in ISO 8601 duration format)

bootstrapAttempts.description: The number of times to check for encrypted realm. Mainly interesting for the first boot during installation.

keychainFileName.description: Absolute file name of the hsm encrypted keychain file.

initContainerProvideKeys.description: Container spec for an init container that provides encryption keys to the keychain generator (for VAU database encryption).

initContainerStoreKeys.description: Container spec for an init container that stores the generated keychain.

keychainGenerator.description: Configuration for the keychain-generator init container (for VAU database encryption).

resources.description: CPU and memory resource requests/limits for the keychain-generator init container.

containerSecurityContext.description: Kubernetes SecurityContext applied to the keychain-generator init container.

extraVolumeMounts.description: Additional volume mounts for the keychain-generator init container.

image.description: Container image overrides for the keychain-generator.

imagePullPolicy.description: Kubernetes image pull policy for the keychain-generator container.

serviceAccount.description: ServiceAccount configuration for the authserver deployment.

podSecurityContext.description: Pod-level security context for the authserver deployment.

truststoresSecretRef.description: If you provide a secret with the truststore password yourself, you can reference it here via its name. It needs the field 'password'.

genesisHashSecretRef.description: If you provide a secret with the genesis hash yourself, you can reference it here via its name. It needs the field 'hash'.

smcbHashingPepperSecretRef.description: If you provide a secret with the smcb hashing pepper yourself, you can reference it here via its name. It needs the field 'pepper'.

secretRef.description: If you provide a secret with 'username' and 'password' fields for the initial keycloak admin account, you can use this by setting its name here.

password.description: Keycloak admin password. Null when managed externally (e.g. via Secret).

maxClients.description: Maximum number of clients per telematik-id.

clientRegistrationTTL.description: Time-to-live for dynamically registered clients in status pending. (This value is in ISO 8601 duration format).

clientRegistrationSchedulerInterval.description: Interval for the scheduler that cleans up pending client registrations that have expired (This value is in ISO 8601 duration format).

containerSecurityContext.description: Kubernetes SecurityContext applied to the authserver container. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/

resources.description: CPU and memory resource requests/limits for the authserver container.

extraVolumeMounts.description: Additional volume mounts for the authserver main container. May be used for truststores, database VAU database encryptions keys or other things.

containerSecurityContext.description: Kubernetes SecurityContext applied to the authserver init container. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/

resources.description: CPU and memory resource requests/limits for the keycloak-build init container.

provisioningProcessor.description: Provisioning processor init container configuration for the authserver deployment.

image.description: Container image overrides for the provisioning processor in authserver deployments.

imagePullPolicy.description: Kubernetes image pull policy for the provisioning processor.

containerSecurityContext.description: Kubernetes SecurityContext applied to the provisioning processor init container. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/

resources.description: CPU and memory resource requests/limits for the provisioning processor in authserver deployments.

genesisHash.description: Initial hash for admin events log.

imagePullPolicy.description: Kubernetes image pull policy for the authserver container.

probes.description: Configurable probe thresholds for the authserver container.

level.description: Keycloak log level. Supports per-category overrides separated by comma.

image.description: Container image overrides for the authserver.

enabled.description: Activate HSM Proxy connection at startup (sets HSM_PROXY_ENDPOINT).

endpoint.description: gRPC address of the HSM Proxy, e.g. "hsm-sim:50051".

tls.description: Pod-level HTTPS via HSM-backed key. Requires hsm.enabled: true.

enabled.description: Enable HTTPS on port 8443 using the HSM-backed HSMPROXY KeyStore.

keyId.description: TLS key ID in the HSM, e.g. "zeta-guard-keycloak-tls-es256-v1.p256".

tokenSigning.description: JWT token signing via HSM-backed ES256 key. Requires hsm.enabled: true.

enabled.description: Enable HSM-backed ES256 token signing (sets HSM_PROXY_TOKEN_KEY_ID).

keyId.description: Token-signing key ID in the HSM, e.g. "zeta-guard-keycloak-token-es256-v1.p256".

dbEnc.description: Application side database encryption via hsm. Requires hsm.enabled: true and dbEnc.enabled: true.

keyId.description: Symmetric key ID in the HSM to use as the key encryption key.

tls.description: Pod-level HTTPS via a TLS cert/key from a Kubernetes Secret.

enabled.description: Enable HTTPS on port 8443 using KC_HTTPS_CERTIFICATE_FILE / KC_HTTPS_CERTIFICATE_KEY_FILE.

certSecretName.description: Name of a kubernetes.io/tls Secret containing tls.crt and tls.key.

hostname.description: External hostname for the Keycloak authserver (passed as --hostname).

minSize.description: Keycloak --db-pool-min-size: minimum number of idle DB connections.

maxSize.description: Keycloak --db-pool-max-size: maximum number of DB connections.

maxThreads.description: Keycloak --http-pool-max-threads: maximum number of Undertow worker threads.

imagePullSecrets.description: Image pull secrets for the authserver container.

smcB.description: SMC-B identity provider settings including OPA integration.

enabled.description: Enable OPA policy enforcement for SMC-B token exchange.

opaBaseUrl.description: Base URL of the active OPA instance. Only for test setups. DO NOT USE IN PRODUCTION!

opaSimulationBaseUrl.description: Base URL of the OPA simulation instance. Empty string disables shadow calls. Only for test setups. DO NOT USE IN PRODUCTION!

decisionPath.description: OPA decision endpoint path. Only for test setups. DO NOT USE IN PRODUCTION!

connectionTimeoutMs.description: HTTP connection timeout in milliseconds for OPA requests.

readTimeoutMs.description: HTTP read timeout in milliseconds for OPA requests.

failClosed.description: When true, deny the token exchange if OPA is unreachable (503). When false, allow. HAS TO BE true IN PRODUCTION

extraVolumes.description: Additional volumes for the authserver pod. May be used for truststores, database VAU database encryptions keys or other things.

adminHostname.description: When set, /auth/admin is blocked via the PEP proxy and a dedicated admin ingress is created. Terraform/CI must use this hostname for keycloak_url.

adminTlsSecretName.description: TLS secret name for the admin ingress. Defaults to 'zeta-guard-admin-tls'. For local/KIND environments reuse the main cert by setting this to 'zeta-guard-tls'.

podLabels.description: Additional labels to add to the authserver pod.

podAnnotations.description: Additional Kubernetes annotations for the authserver pod.

hostAlias.description: Adds an entry to the authserver pod's /etc/hosts, mapping hostAlias.ip to hostAlias.name. Mostly for test setup purposes.

ip.description: IP address for the host alias.

name.description: Hostname to map to the IP.

affinity.description: Kubernetes affinity rules for authserver pod scheduling.

tolerations.description: Kubernetes tolerations for authserver pod scheduling.

authServerMetricsEnabled.description: Enable the Prometheus metrics endpoint for Keycloak.

authServerTracingEnabled.description: Enable OpenTelemetry distributed tracing for Keycloak.

additionalLabels.description: Map of labels added to each zeta-guard resource.

pepproxyMetricsExporterImage.description: Container image for the nginx-prometheus-exporter sidecar. Required when pepproxyMetricsEnabled is true.

gematik.description: Configuration for the gematik connection (OIDC token renewal via Workload Identity Federation).

idTokenAudience.description: Audience claim for the ID token generated by the gematik-oidc-token-renewer cronjob. Typically the OAuth 2.0 Client ID of the IAP-protected BDE data receiver.

serviceAccountEmailAddress.description: Google service account email to impersonate via Workload Identity Federation.

workloadIdentityFederation.description: Google Workload Identity Federation settings for the gematik OIDC token renewer.

projectNumber.description: GCP project number that contains the workload identity pool.

gematikConnectionEnabled.description: When true, deploy the CronJob that maintains a valid gematik OIDC access token.

global.description: Global values shared across all subcharts.

imagePullSecrets.description: Image pull secrets applied globally to all pods.

registry_host.description: Container registry host prefix prepended to all image references.

imageTrustCertchainSecretRef.description: Secret name that has a field 'certchain.pem' with gematik's certchain for container image signatures (only CA and intermediate certs, no leaf cert).

opaDistributedTracingEnabled.description: Enable OPA distributed tracing via a gRPC OpenTelemetry endpoint.

opaStatusPrometheus.description: Export OPA status (bundle and plugin) metrics to Prometheus.

databaseMode.description: Whether to use your own database (external) or CloudNativePG (cloudnative) as the authserver db.

authserverDb.description: Relevant only for databaseMode=external. This is how you can configure your own DB.

kcDb.description: See environment variable KC_DB in keycloak config https://www.keycloak.org/server/db

kcDbUrl.description: See environment variable KC_DB_URL in keycloak config https://www.keycloak.org/server/db

kcDbSecretName.description: Name of a secret that contains the database username and password in its fields `username` and 'password'. Will be passed into environment variables KC_DB_USERNAME and KC_DB_PASSWORD. See keycloak config https://www.keycloak.org/server/db

kcDbSchema.description: See environment variable KC_DB_SCHEMA in keycloak config https://www.keycloak.org/server/db

cloudnativeDbUrl.description: JDBC URL for the CloudNativePG database connection (databaseMode=cloudnative).

cloudnativeDbSecretName.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret containing username and password for the CloudNativePG database (databaseMode=cloudnative).

cloudnativeDbSchema.description: Database schema for the CloudNativePG database (databaseMode=cloudnative).

cloudnativePg.description: Relevant only for databaseMode=cloudnative. Configures the managed CloudNativePG cluster. For details see https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/charts/blob/main/charts/cluster/README.md

serviceAccount.description: ServiceAccount configuration for the PEP proxy deployment.

podSecurityContext.description: Pod-level security context for the PEP proxy deployment.

containerSecurityContext.description: Kubernetes SecurityContext applied to the pep container. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/

wellKnownBase.description: Base URL used to construct .well-known endpoints for token validation. This is your public url.

asl_enabled.description: Enable ASL in the PEP proxy.

aslRootCA.description: Root CA name for ASL certificate chain validation.

aslOcsp.description: Enable or disable OCSP Stapling for ASL. Alternatively provide a OCSP responder URL to enable OCSP stapling whilst overriding the OCSP responder URLs from the certificates.

aslOcspTtl.description: Maximum validity duration of cached OCSP responses (default 24h per A_24624-01).

image.description: Container image overrides for the PEP proxy.

imagePullSecrets.description: Image pull secrets for the pepproxy container.

nginxConf.description: nginx configuration for the PEP proxy.

noTravel.description: Enable or disable no-travel enforcement in the PEP proxy. THIS MUST BE ENABLED IN PRODUCTION.

configMapName.description: Name of the ConfigMap holding the Nginx configuration.

fachdienstUrl.description: You COULD use this in your `pepproxy.locations` block.

generateConfigMap.description: When true, the chart generates the nginx ConfigMap from values. When false, use an existing ConfigMap. WARNING: Use with caution since this silently conflicts with basically most of PEP's configuration.

httpClientAcceptInvalidCerts.description: When true, the PEP proxy accepts invalid upstream TLS certificates. DO NOT SET TO true IN PRODUCTION.

aslTestmode.description: Enable ASL test mode. Disables encryption. DO NOT SET TO true IN PRODUCTION.

locations.description: Additional Nginx location blocks injected into the generated config. Here you should configure connection to your Fachdienst.

pepIssuer.description: OIDC issuer (iss) of the authorization server used for access token validation. This is used for token validation as well as for retvrieving the JWKS and similar things.

anyOf.description: JWKS issuer URL for PoPP key validation.

poppValidity.description:
quarter: within the current quarter (see gemSpec_ZETA — A_26477)
duration string: fixed validity period, since iat + optional unit (d,h,m,s); defaults to s if no unit given.

requiredAudience.description: Required audience (aud) claim in access tokens. This should be your public Fachdienst URL.

requiredScopes.description: Scopes required for a valid access token. This should be defined in your Fachdienst Spec.

resources.description: CPU and memory resource requests/limits for the pepproxy container.

imagePullPolicy.description: Kubernetes image pull policy for the PEP proxy container.

podLabels.description: Additional labels to add to the pepproxy pod.

podAnnotations.description: Additional Kubernetes annotations for the pepproxy pod.

hsmProxyAddr.description: gRPC URL of HSM Proxy to enable ossl_hsm integration in the PEP proxy. Set to null to disable.

affinity.description: Kubernetes affinity rules for pepproxy pod scheduling.

tolerations.description: Kubernetes tolerations for pepproxy pod scheduling.

provisioningProcessor.description: Provisioning processor sidecar that handles trust anchor provisioning.

containerSecurityContext.description: Kubernetes SecurityContext applied to the provisioningProcessor container. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/

image.description: Container image overrides for the provisioning processor.

imagePullPolicy.description: Kubernetes image pull policy for the provisioning processor.

provisioningContainer.description: Name of the provisioning container image. Use to switch between RU/PU environments or a custom mirror.

provisioningContainerCaSecretRef.description: Reference to a Kubernetes Secret containing the PEM-encoded CA certificate for the provisioning container registry. The secret is mounted as /var/registry-ca/ca.crt in each init container and sets PROVISIONING_CONTAINER_REGISTRY_CA_FILE accordingly.

key.description: Key within the Secret that holds the PEM certificate data.

resources.description: CPU and memory resource requests/limits for the provisioning processor.

hsmsim.description: HSM simulator used for local/test cryptographic operations. DO NOT USE IN PRODUCTION!

resources.description: CPU and memory resource requests/limits for the HSM simulator.

ingress.description: Ingress controller settings for the zeta-guard services.

enabled.description: Enable ip-hash sticky sessions via NIC (hash $http_x_forwarded_for). Required when replicaCount > 1.

pepproxyMetricsEnabled.description: Enable the Prometheus metrics exporter sidecar for the PEP proxy.

pepproxyTracingEnabled.description: Enable OpenTelemetry distributed tracing for the PEP proxy.

telemetry-gateway.description: OpenTelemetry Collector deployment that aggregates and exports telemetry data. Configured via the opentelemetry-collector subchart.

telemetryGatewayEnabled.description: Include the telemetry gateway in the Helm release. SHOULD BE true FOR PRODUCTION.

opa.description: Open Policy Agent (OPA) configuration for authorization policy evaluation.

bundle.description: OPA bundle mode configuration for loading policies from a remote registry.

enabled.description: Enable OPA bundle mode. When false, inline policyRego is used.

serviceName.description: Logical service name used in OPA config to identify the bundle source.

url.description: Base URL of the OCI/Docker registry serving bundles.

resource.description: Fully qualified bundle resource path including registry host.

min_delay_seconds.description: Minimum polling delay in seconds between bundle download attempts.

max_delay_seconds.description: Maximum polling delay in seconds between bundle download attempts.

enabled.description: Enable cryptographic signature verification for downloaded bundles. THIS SHOULD BE true IN PRODUCTION!

keyId.description: Key ID that must match the signing key embedded in the bundle. You can usually leave this alone and rely on the default.

algorithm.description: Signature algorithm used to verify the bundle. You can usually leave this alone and rely on the default.

publicKey.description: PEM-encoded public key for bundle signature verification. DO NOT TOUCH FOR PRODUCTION!

scope.description: Must match the scope embedded in the bundle signature. Omit if the bundle was signed without a scope.

secretRef.description: Reference to a Kubernetes Secret holding registry bearer credentials.

name.description: Name of the Secret in the release namespace. Empty string disables credential injection (e.g. in WIF mode).

containerSecurityContext.description: Kubernetes SecurityContext applied to the trust-anchor provisioning processor init container in OPA deployments. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/

resources.description: CPU and memory resource requests/limits for the provisioning processor in OPA deployments.

containerSecurityContext.description: Kubernetes SecurityContext applied to the opa container. See https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/

cronjobSecurityContext.description: Kubernetes SecurityContext applied to the OPA-related CronJob containers (e.g. token renewer). See https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/

serviceAccountName.description: Kubernetes ServiceAccount name for OPA pods. Required for Workload Identity Federation.

image.description: Container image overrides for opa deployments.

imagePullPolicy.description: Kubernetes image pull policy for the OPA container.

imagePullSecrets.description: Image pull secrets for the OPA container.

resources.description: CPU and memory resource requests/limits for the OPA container.

workloadIdentityFederation.description: Workload Identity Federation for accessing Google Artifact Registry without static credentials.

enabled.description: Enable Workload Identity Federation for OPA bundle downloads from GAR.

audience.description: STS audience (the WIF provider resource name).

sa.description: Google service account email to impersonate.

tokenRenewer.description: CronJob that periodically refreshes the WIF access token.

schedule.description: Cron schedule expression for token renewal.

backoffLimit.description: Number of retries for failed token renewal Jobs.

simulation.description: OPA simulation instance for shadow policy evaluation.

enabled.description: Deploy a second OPA instance that evaluates policies in shadow mode.

bundle.description: Bundle configuration specific to the simulation OPA instance.

resource.description: Bundle resource path for the simulation instance.

enabled.description: Enable or disable the OPA deployment.

podLabels.description: Additional labels to add to the OPA pod.

podAnnotations.description: Additional Kubernetes annotations for the OPA pod.

affinity.description: Kubernetes affinity rules for OPA pod scheduling.

tolerations.description: Kubernetes tolerations for OPA pod scheduling.

ingressMasterAnnotations.description: Extra annotations added to the master Ingress resource.

ingressMinionAnnotations.description: Extra annotations added to minion (path-based) Ingress resources.

routeViaTigerProxy.description: When true, the Ingress routes traffic through the tiger-proxy service instead of directly exposing internal services. DO NOT USE IN PRODUCTION

ingressClassName.description: IngressClass used by generated Ingress resources. Must match the NIC class name if the bundled NIC is enabled.

clusterIssuer.description: cert-manager ClusterIssuer name used for TLS certificates on Ingress resources.

openshiftIngress.description: OpenShift Ingress Route-Controller with TLS. Creates edge-terminated routes with TLS redirect via OpenShift's Ingress-to-Route controller. nginxIngressEnabled should be false

enabled.description: When true, add TLS configuration to Ingress resources for OpenShift's route controller.

certName.description: Name of the TLS Secret referenced in Ingress TLS blocks.

nginxIngressEnabled.description: When true, deploy the bundled NGINX Ingress Controller and add specific annotations for this ingress controller.

nginxIngressHsm.description: When true, disable spec.tls of the master ingress to enable custom TLS configuration via ingressMasterAnnotations."nginx.org/server-snippets", as required for ossl_hsm support.

nginx-ingress.description: Configuration passed to the bundled nginx-ingress subchart.

name.description: Name of the IngressClass resource to be monitored by the ingress controller. Relevant for namespace isolation.

pod.description: Settings applied to the NIC controller pod template.

annotations.description: Annotations on the NIC controller pod template. Changing any value triggers a rolling restart.

entries.description: Key-value map of NGINX configuration directives.

ssl-ecdh-curve.description: Allowed ECDH curves for TLS key exchange.

ssl-session-tickets.description: Enable or disable TLS session tickets.

ssl-prefer-server-ciphers.description: Prefer server cipher order over client preference.

enableCustomResources.description: Install ingress controller's custom resource definitions (VirtualServer, etc.). Disable if only using standard Ingress. Not required for ZETA.

service.description: Kubernetes Service configuration for the ingress controller.

type.description: Service type for the ingress controller.

externalTrafficPolicy.description: External traffic policy for the ingress controller Service.

devMode.description: When true, inject a changing annotation on each helm upgrade to force pod rollouts even if the image tag is unchanged. May/will lead to unneccesary restarts.

registry_name.description: Registry path segment appended after global.registry_host for zeta-guard images.

networkPolicy.description: Egress NetworkPolicy configuration (A_27864-01). When enabled, each ZETA Guard pod receives an egress NetworkPolicy restricting outbound traffic to the explicitly listed IP blocks. Internal cluster traffic (DNS, pod-to-pod) is always allowed.

enabled.description: Enable egress NetworkPolicies for all ZETA Guard pods.

egress.description: Egress rule sets, one per external destination category.

artifactRegistry.description: A_27864-01 — ZETA Artifact Registry at gematik (image pulls, OPA bundles).

ocspCabForum.description: A_27864-01 — OCSP/CRL for TLS TSPs per CAB Forum.

ocspTiPki.description: A_27864-01 — OCSP Responder TSP Komponenten PKI der TI.

pip.description: PIP — source of OPA policy bundles.

mock.description: Allow egress to the local popp-mock pod via podSelector (app=popp-mock) on targetPort 8080. For use with the popp-mocks subchart in local KIND stages only.

providerInternal.description: Provider specific (German anbieterspezifisch) egress rules for resource servers, telemetry systems, and cluster-local pods.

allowAll.description: When true, all egress is permitted without IP filtering (overrides all other providerInternal rules). Use only in controlled environments.

resourceServers.description: IP blocks for provider-internal resource servers (e.g. JWK fetch endpoint via ingress). Use HOST_IP/32 for local KIND stages.

telemetrySystems.description: IP blocks for provider-internal telemetry systems.

podSelectors.description: Allow egress to cluster-local pods by label selector (post-DNAT, kindnet pattern). Use for test-only cluster-local backends such as testfachdienst in local KIND stages.

matchLabels.description: Label selector that identifies the target pods.

port.description: Target port (post-DNAT) on the destination pod. Must be the container port, not the Service port.

ZetaImage.description: Container image reference with optional registry override and digest pinning.

registry.description: Registry override for this specific image.

K8sImagePullSecrets.description: List of Kubernetes image pull secret references.

name.description: Name of the referenced Kubernetes resource.

K8sResourceRequirements.description: Compute resources required by this container.

limits.description: Maximum compute resources the container may consume.

requests.description: Minimum compute resources guaranteed to the container.

K8sPodSecurityContext.description: Pod-level security context. Applied to all containers in the pod.

create.description: Create a dedicated ServiceAccount for this workload.

name.description: Name of the ServiceAccount to use.

initialDelaySeconds.description: Number of seconds after the container has started before the probe is initiated.

periodSeconds.description: How often (in seconds) to perform the probe.

failureThreshold.description: Number of consecutive failures before the probe is considered failed.

timeoutSeconds.description: Number of seconds after which the probe times out.

successThreshold.description: Minimum consecutive successes for the probe to be considered successful after having failed.

K8sPodDisruptionBudget.description: PodDisruptionBudget configuration. Either minAvailable or maxUnavailable can be set, but not both.

enabled.description: Create a PodDisruptionBudget for this workload.

minAvailable.description: Minimum number/percentage of pods that must remain available.

maxUnavailable.description: Maximum number/percentage of pods that can be unavailable.

NetPolIpBlockList.description: List of CIDR blocks for a NetworkPolicy egress rule.

items.description: A CIDR block in prefix/length notation.

NetPolIpBlockRule.description: Egress rule containing a list of CIDR blocks for one external destination category.
